executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. Qilin ransomware leads the attack wave with 20 victims, followed by SafePay (11), Play (10), and Clop (13). High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit twice by CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, Peterborough Public Health, and the Rural Municipality of Gimli. Critical infrastructure sectors affected include healthcare, education, public sector, telecommunications, and financial services. This represents a coordinated multi-threat-actor campaign requiring immediate defensive action across all Canadian organizations.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical national cybersecurity emergency with 103 organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. Healthcare infrastructure is under direct attack (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Thunder Bay Counselling, BioPharma Services, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Labtician Ophthalmics), alongside municipal governments (Rural Municipality of Gimli, Municipality of North Perth), educational institutions (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, teccart.qc.ca), and critical telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose breached by two separate threat actors (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) and Kids & Company (childcare services) also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting/financial firms hit including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and Bergmanis Preyra law firm.

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco ASA and Firepower devices are under active exploitation via three N-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363), prompting an official alert from Canada's Cyber Centre (CCCS). Multiple supply chain attacks are unfolding: the npm ecosystem was compromised with worm-like malware targeting developers, and Bitwarden's CLI tool was hijacked to steal credentials and SSH keys. The UK Biobank suffered a major breach exposing health data of 500,000 individuals now listed for sale in China. North Korea's Lazarus group continues macOS targeting via ClickFix social engineering, while the threat actor ShinyHunters claims breaches of Udemy, ADT, and Carnival Corporation affecting millions.

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning of active exploitation of three Cisco ASA and Firepower vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363). This represents the most significant official Canadian government cybersecurity advisory in the reporting period. No major Canadian financial institutions or retailers were identified in breach reports during this timeframe.
